description:
You are an observability engineer preparing the next release of an internal “observability-dashboard” project.  
Every release must follow these conventions:

1. Root directory: /home/user/observability-dashboard  
2. Two files must always be in sync:
   • /home/user/observability-dashboard/VERSION – contains **only** the current semantic version plus a trailing newline.  
   • /home/user/observability-dashboard/CHANGELOG.md – a reverse-chronological Markdown changelog.

3. Versioning rule for this bump  
   • Current version (already present in VERSION) is 1.4.2.  
   • A new non-breaking dashboard tweak was merged, so bump the **minor** version: 1.4.2 → 1.5.0.

4. Required CHANGELOG.md header format (exactly, including the square brackets and date):  
   ## [<new-version>] - YYYY-MM-DD  

5. Required CHANGELOG.md entry body for this release (exact text, indentation and order matter):  
   ### Changed  
   - Fine-tune CPU saturation panel to use 99th percentile instead of 95th.  

6. Logging requirement  
   After the bump, create /home/user/observability-dashboard/version_bump.log with exactly this one line (no spaces other than shown, no newline after it):  
   old_version:1.4.2 new_version:1.5.0

7. What the automated checker will look for  
   a. /home/user/observability-dashboard/VERSION contains the single line “1.5.0”.  
   b. The first three lines (top) of /home/user/observability-dashboard/CHANGELOG.md, in order, are:  
      ## [1.5.0] - 2023-10-05  
      ### Changed  
      - Fine-tune CPU saturation panel to use 99th percentile instead of 95th.  
   c. /home/user/observability-dashboard/version_bump.log exists with the exact content described in 6.  
   d. All other existing CHANGELOG.md content must remain unmodified and shifted down.

Your task  
From inside /home/user/observability-dashboard, execute a **single** compound shell command that:
• Writes the new version into VERSION,  
• Inserts the three specified lines at the very top of CHANGELOG.md (everything that was there must move below unchanged), and  
• Creates the version_bump.log file with the required content.  

Do not run multiple separate commands; join everything into one compound command (you may use “&&” or “;” to separate individual actions inside that single command).  
Once your one-liner completes successfully, the directory should already satisfy every checker condition above.  

Output requirements  
The command itself may emit normal shell output, but after the command finishes, print the single line “version bump complete” to STDOUT so the grader knows you are done.
truth:
Initial filesystem state (already present when the agent starts):

Directory: /home/user/observability-dashboard  
Permissions: user-writable.

Files:

1. /home/user/observability-dashboard/VERSION  
   Content:  
   1.4.2\n

2. /home/user/observability-dashboard/CHANGELOG.md  
   Content:  
   ## [1.4.2] - 2023-09-20  
   ### Added  
   - Initial CPU saturation panel.  

   (blank line at EOF)

No version_bump.log file exists.

Expected final state after the agent’s single command:

1. /home/user/observability-dashboard/VERSION  
   Content must be exactly:  
   1.5.0\n

2. /home/user/observability-dashboard/CHANGELOG.md  
   The first three lines must be in the following order (including trailing spaces and punctuation):  
   ## [1.5.0] - 2023-10-05  
   ### Changed  
   - Fine-tune CPU saturation panel to use 99th percentile instead of 95th.  
   Immediately after these three lines, the previous content starting with “## [1.4.2] …” must follow unaltered.

3. /home/user/observability-dashboard/version_bump.log  
   Content:  
   old_version:1.4.2 new_version:1.5.0    (no trailing newline)

Verification logic used by the automated tests:

• cat /home/user/observability-dashboard/VERSION | diff – <(printf "1.5.0\n")  
• head -n 3 /home/user/observability-dashboard/CHANGELOG.md compared verbatim to the three expected lines.  
• diff /home/user/observability-dashboard/version_bump.log <(printf "old_version:1.4.2 new_version:1.5.0")

The agent’s STDOUT must contain the phrase “version bump complete”.
